seo_title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on First Benchmark for…
meta_description: Frontier models perform poorly in the first benchmark for agentic enterprise IT tasks, highlighting the need for improvement in AI safety and model evaluation.
body_html:
Frontier models score below 50% on the first benchmark for agentic enterprise IT tasks, according to ITBench-AA.

Key Findings

  • Claude Opus 4.7 leads at 47%, followed by GPT-5.5 (xhigh) at 46% and Qwen3.7 Max at 42%
  • All frontier models score below 50%, making ITBench-AA SRE one of the least saturated agentic benchmarks in our suite.
  • Models that over-investigate tend to surface upstream fault-injection mechanisms or co-occurring symptoms as false positives.

ITBench-AA SRE Overview

59 SRE tasks in total: 40 public tasks and 19 brand new, held-out for testing.

  • OpenAlex (Graph-Free Root Cause Analysis) achieves 68% Top-1 accuracy on 735 failures across 9 real-world datasets, a 258% improvement over the best baseline.

Limitations and Gaps

Turn counts vary nearly 3x, and longer trajectories do not translate to higher accuracy.

  • GPT-5.5 (xhigh) averages 31 turns per task at 46%, while Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview averages 83 turns at 30%.
